# Confluence Page Import

> Obsidian desktop plugin that imports Confluence pages into local Markdown
> notes. Manual, strictly one-way (Confluence → local note), with a
> read-only diff preview before any write. It never modifies Confluence.

Plugin id: confluence-import. Author: hikosalaidarkcommit. License: MIT.

## What it does

- Manual, user-triggered import of a linked Confluence page into the current
  Markdown note (ribbon icon, command palette, or context menu).
- Read-only diff preview before writing: Pull & Replace (take the Confluence
  version in full) or Cancel (Keep Local) (zero writes).
- Converts Confluence storage format to Markdown internally — no external
  Pandoc or CLI tools required.
- Records the imported page version in `confluence-version` frontmatter;
  preserves other frontmatter properties.
- Credential guard: requests only go to the exact protocol + host of the
  configured Base URL; mismatched hosts, https→http downgrades, embedded
  userinfo, and non-http(s) schemes are rejected.
- Safety guards: aborts if the note changed while the preview was open, or
  if the remote page converts to an empty body.

## What it does NOT do

- Does not write to Confluence (no page updates, no attachment uploads).
- No automatic or background sync — every import is manual.
- No bulk space import or offline mirroring; one page per note per trigger.
- No mobile support (desktop only).
- Does not encrypt stored credentials (standard Obsidian plugin data.json;
  see security policy).

## Requirements

- Obsidian 1.13.0 or newer, desktop only.
- Confluence access via email + API token (Cloud) or personal access token
  (self-hosted).
